claimText: Gokul Reservoir is an oxbow lake located in Bihar and serves as an important habitat for the Blackbuck.
evidenceExcerpt: Gokul Reservoir (Buxar, Bihar) is an oxbow lake and an important habitat for Blackbuck.
examUse: Correct-pair questions matching wetlands to their states or key fauna.
distractorHooks: Located in Uttar Pradesh or Madhya Pradesh
Habitat for One-horned Rhino or Sangai
Is a crater lake or tectonic lake

claimText: Gogabeel Lake is the first community reserve of Bihar and is formed from the flow of the Mahananda, Kankhar, and Ganga rivers.
evidenceExcerpt: Gogabeel Lake (Katihar, Bihar) is the 1st community reserve of Bihar, formed from the flow of Mahananda, Kankhar, and Ganga rivers.
examUse: Statement-based questions on wetland formation and conservation status.
distractorHooks: First conservation reserve instead of community reserve
Formed by Kosi or Gandak rivers

claimText: Vembanad Lake is the largest brackish, humid tropical wetland ecosystem in Kerala.
evidenceExcerpt: Vembanad Lake (Kerala) is the largest brackish, humid tropical wetland ecosystem in Kerala, and its surface area shrank by 27% between 1917 and 1990.
examUse: Statement-based questions on the ecological characteristics of major Indian lakes.
distractorHooks: Located in Tamil Nadu or Andhra Pradesh
Is the smallest wetland in Kerala

claimText: Dal Lake in Jammu and Kashmir is fed by the Arrah river and is divided into Gagribal, Lokut Dal, Bod Dal, and Nagin basins.
evidenceExcerpt: Dal Lake (J&K) hosted the first Khelo India water games, is fed by the Arrah river, and is divided into Gagribal, Lokut Dal, Bod Dal, and Nagin basins.
examUse: Statement-based questions on the hydrology and geography of Himalayan lakes.
distractorHooks: Fed directly by the Jhelum or Chenab rivers
Divided into Wular or Manasbal basins
